skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Routes image-to-video requests to documented RunComfy models. It helps choose HappyHorse, Wan 2.7, or Seedance based on animation, lip-sync, and reference-media needs.
skill.summary: Choose and invoke the right RunComfy image-to-video workflow for a still image.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-crypto alerts are largely false positives caused by Markdown code spans, schemas, and examples in SKILL.md. The real risks are documented external CLI execution, third-party media URL processing through RunComfy, and local token storage, so publication should include a clear network and credential warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to install or run RunComfy CLI commands. This is expected for the skill, but it can execute local binaries and submit jobs to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the skill. The examples use structured JSON inputs, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of command injection.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Media Submission
medium_findings.description: The skill sends image, audio, and video URLs to RunComfy model endpoints. User media and prompts may leave the local environment and be processed by a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly states that the CLI posts to the Model API and that media URLs are fetched by RunComfy. This confirms external processing without showing malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Credential Storage and Environment Token Use
medium_findings.description: The skill documents RunComfy login and token storage under the user home directory, or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN for CI. This is normal authentication, but users must protect the token.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file names the token location and environment variable explicitly. It also documents owner-only file permissions, reducing but not removing credential-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick findings, but the reviewed locations are Markdown inline code, fenced command examples, model names, or schema field names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, not Ruby code. The backtick characters delimit examples and field names rather than executing commands.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URL and Path Examples
low_findings.description: Path traversal and hardcoded URL alerts come from placeholder media URLs and output directory placeholders. No file-reading behavior or traversal logic is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples contain ellipsis placeholders and user-supplied output directories. I found no code that normalizes paths, opens arbitrary files, or appends traversal sequences.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts do not match confirmed cryptographic operations or host reconnaissance. They appear to be keyword matches in prose, schema tables, and exit-code documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, or system discovery code in SKILL.md. The cited lines are documentation text rather than executable logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on npx installation and runcomfy CLI execution. Users should review commands before running them and avoid passing untrusted shell-expanded values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and intentional. The documentation uses quoted JSON examples, which lowers injection confidence but not the external-command risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Processing Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill submits requests to RunComfy and downloads generated output URLs. This creates expected outbound network traffic for media generation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is described directly in the security section. It is expected service behavior, not hidden network activity.

content.value_statement: Choosing the right image-to-video model can waste time and credits. This skill routes each request to a RunComfy model with matching prompts and inputs.
actual_capabilities: Routes general image animation requests to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V.
Routes custom voiceover lip-sync requests to Wan 2.7 with audio_url.
Routes multi-reference image, video, and audio requests to Seedance 2.0 Pro.
Provides field requirements for image_url, audio_url, duration, resolution, and aspect ratio.
Includes example RunComfy CLI invocations for each supported route.
Documents prompting tips for motion, camera movement, lighting, and reference consistency.
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account, CLI installation, and authentication token.
Submits prompts and media URLs to RunComfy for remote processing.
Does not combine multiple model routes in a single generation.
Output length, resolution, and media requirements depend on the selected model.

use_cases.title: Create social video from a product image
use_cases.description: A marketer can animate a product photo with a short camera move and stable packaging details.

use_cases.title: Generate localized talking-head clips
use_cases.description: A video producer can pair a visual prompt with different voiceover files for language variants.

use_cases.title: Build reference-guided concept clips
use_cases.description: A designer can combine subject images, scene videos, and audio references for a controlled concept preview.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a single image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple motion clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this image URL into a short video. Use gentle camera movement, stable identity, and natural light. Choose the best RunComfy route.

prompt_templates.title: Create a product reveal
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with product preservation needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product image as a five-second reveal. Keep the label and shape unchanged. Use a slow orbit and clean studio lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Make a voiceover lip-sync clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a vertical talking-head video using this voiceover URL. Match duration to the audio and avoid subtitles, flicker, and distorted hands.

prompt_templates.title: Use multiple reference assets
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with image, video, and audio references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image 1 for the subject, video 1 for scene movement, and audio 1 for voice tone. Create a cohesive eight-second concept clip.

output_examples.input: A portrait image URL with a request to make the person subtly move.
output_examples.output: The skill selects HappyHorse 1.0 I2V, prepares a motion-focused prompt, and recommends a short identity-stable animation.

output_examples.input: A spokesperson prompt plus an MP3 voiceover URL for a vertical ad.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Wan 2.7, sets audio_url, matches duration to the audio, and uses a talking-head prompt.

output_examples.input: A character image, a cafe reference video, and a short voice reference.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Seedance 2.0 Pro and describes how each reference should guide the generated clip.

best_practices: Review every generated RunComfy command before execution.
Use trusted media URLs and avoid sensitive images or private voice recordings unless approved.
Describe motion, camera, lighting, and preservation goals in concrete terms.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential media to third-party services without permission.
Do not ask for several unrelated motion beats in one short clip.
Do not force a lip-sync request through a route that lacks audio_url support.

faq.question: Does this skill generate video locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI and RunComfy model APIs for remote generation.

faq.question: Which model is used by default?
faq.answer: The skill recommends HappyHorse 1.0 I2V for general portrait, product, and image animation requests.

faq.question: Can it lip-sync to my own audio?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes custom voiceover requests to Wan 2.7 with an audio_url input.

faq.question: Can it use reference video and audio together?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes multi-reference requests to Seedance 2.0 Pro when image, video, and audio references are needed.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: A RunComfy account is required. The CLI uses login storage or the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: What should users check before running it?
faq.answer: Users should verify media URLs, output paths, token handling, and the selected model route before executing commands.
